text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.
translated: Hear ye, the young who always do good deeds! Now look, you great ones that I have! But they do not truly please my heart, and I have much sorrow within me.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.
translated: Nyaso did this deed to me, saying, “Father! You, Great Chief Onanto Lékpo, have done this deed many times, oh-oh,” and said, “You are indeed great,” so they listened.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.
translated: Nya Petro said to the people, "Chief Ekpe said let us build houses for the people and our people; Onanto said to you, do not be lazy, let us help the people together!" Then the message quickly spread through the words of all the people, they heard.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Se Oka Herodes díkpǝ eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte Yosef lǝ kola kǝmiǝ lǝ Egipte.
translated: When King Herod heard about the child, he commanded that all the male children be killed, and Joseph went with Mary and the child to Egypt.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝ dimǝ laanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ.
translated: We speak only these tales about the people of our homes. Listen, wise ones, now hear these true sayings from the mouth.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.
translated: Listen, the young people utter empty oaths! But they see that I have no important things! Because they say I break oaths, and I lack the strength to uphold them.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.
translated: Nyaso and the people told me, “Hey! Our Chief Onanto and the people are having a great fight oh-oh,” and said “We’re having a small fight,” but they were lying.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.
translated: Nya Petro addressed the people, “Chief, let us speak about these people who are not doing good deeds; Onanto, you cause trouble by not doing good deeds!” Then the people listened attentively to what was said, truly.
